{{Quote|Flandon Sweeg was a dangerous man, a spacer who, like many others, sometimes resorted to dishonest ways of keeping body and soul together.|''The Slug Named Grendel'' by Rogar Farnoster|<ref name="Grendel" />}}
'''Flandon Sweeg''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] character in [[Rogar Farnoster]]'s ''[[The Slug Named Grendel]]'', published by [[TriPlanetary Press/Legends|TriPlanetary Press]]<ref name="Grendel" /> sometime after [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].<ref name="HNN-54">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|54|life/1352_2.html|Giant Space Slug Found}}</ref> Sweeg was the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Darkfire]]'', and was regarded by [[Sosakar]], the narrator, as a dangerous [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] who ruled by force rather than affection, earning him little love from his crew. Sweeg, deeply in debt with repossession agents tailing him, decided to recoup his wealth by capturing the legendary [[Exogorth/Legends|space slug]] [[Grendel (space slug)|Grendel]], whose million-plus [[Kilogram/Legends|kilogram]] body would be worth a fortune at the going rate of a thousand [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] per kilo. His crew abandoned him, realizing that they'd be facing the treacherous [[Borkeen Belt]] where Grendel lived for nothing if Grendel was only a legend, or facing the monstrous ship-eating space slug itself if the legend was true. Nonetheless, Sweeg pressed on, with the naïve and debt-ridden Sosakar signed on as crew.<ref name="Grendel" />

==Behind the scenes==
The [[The Slug Named Grendel|work]] Flandon Sweeg appeared in bears strong similarities with the 1851 novel [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moby_Dick Moby Dick], authored by [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Herman_Melville Herman Melville]. Such similarities includes the formatting of the first line and the narrative of the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ishmael_(Moby-Dick) protagonist] who has joined a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pequod_(Moby-Dick) crew] upon a quest led by a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Captain_Ahab captain] of questionable morals after a sizable [[Limb/Legends|limbless]], [[Starship/Legends|ship]]-destroying [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moby_Dick_(whale) creature] [[Color/Legends|colored]] white.
